items.title: Long time no write...
items.summary: Well there has not been much happening. Krabi River marina is a bit falling apart but its location is wonderful. I do a six month deal with the new management. So now I am walking distance of all the shops and restaurants in Krabi. And some interesting bugs for sale by the roadside. In the meantime I manage to renew my visa for another 12 months; never a st…

items.title: Moving around Krabi
items.summary: In my last post I mentioned that the Moken children on Phayam had to walk to school as the footbridge still had not been repaired after the Tsunami. Well here is a photograph to prove it. And I also spoke of the drugged speedboat driver who managed to hit a cliff and kill a five year old girl. Here is a photograph of what he hit. How drugged out do you have…

items.title: Ko Phayam and back to Krabi
items.summary: Life is quiet on the island. The Moken villagers are penniless but don't seem to care. I decide to be generous and give them good money to dive under my boat to clean the bottom. They are utterly useless. Fiona who lives on the island organises a quiz to raise money to bring vets in to round up and castrate feral dogs. These dogs are a bit of a menace. Mean…

items.title: The Moken village
items.summary: I arrive at the crossing point for the Moken Village. The bridges are gone and the raft looks decidedly dodgy but the ferry captain assures me that if I sit on the port bow I will balance out the missing float, starboard aft. I don't have much choice if I want to get there. The Mokens are sea gypsies originating from many different Asian countries and hence…

items.title: A few Christmas snaps
items.summary: With few boats there are just nine of us for Christmas lunch. Tiny little fishing boats bring in the goodies. We bring our own wine to the resort and are not charged any corkage. An American with his Chinese family amazes us with his bi-lingual 26 month old daughter...

items.title: Getting to Ko Phayam
items.summary: I head out of Krabi Boat Lagoon into the middle of the jellyfish catching season. A man in the bow of a longtail has a net on a long pole and directs the skipper towards a jellyfish. The skipper changes course without looking so life is exciting for me. But this is big business for Krabi. I am so busy dodging I forget to take photographs. I leave Chicken Is…

items.title: Off to Ko Phyam for Christmas
items.summary: Well having landed back in Thailand I clear in, a proposed crew member malfunctions and I head to the peace of Krabi Boat Lagoon. Here I plan my new year refit without having a plan as to who is going to help me do it. In any event I am heading off to Ko Phyam on the border with Myanmar for Christmas. About 160 nautical miles with the probability of very li…

items.title: Satun
items.summary: I jump on the ferry, five minutes walk from the marina to complete my obligatory visa run. Less than 90 minutes takes me to Satun in Thailand. Against all the odds the hotel staff are waiting for me at the terminal with my car. Next day I head off to PSS shipyard to see Des and Ked Kearns who are just about to finish the rebuild of Cariad. The owner was the…

items.title: Visa run to Satun
items.summary: My friend Gavin in the local walking group owns Sailor's Gourmet foods and one key product is his lamb and rosemary sausages. A breakfast favourite! Things are much quieter in the marina. Long distance cruising still has not recovered from Covid and there are 32 empty berths on my pontoon alone. And another space comes free as a derelict catamaran is towed…

items.title: Quick run to Penang
items.summary: I head off to Penang for my annual medical check up. All seems OK and I have booked into a nice boutique hotel which is walking distance from most places. The boat is left behind as the marina is uncomfortable at this time of year. And also more expensive to put in the marina than my boutique hotel! I take the funicular railway to the top of the hill and ha…

items.title: Off to Langkawi
items.summary: Long time no write because there has not been much to write about. After the fire at Krabi River I moved to Krabi Boat Lagoon, very secure and sheltered but miles from anywhere. The marina is being dredged but somehow they do not get it quite right with some parts very deep and others too shallow. The rainy season has kicked in and so I am off again to Lang…

items.title: Too good to last
items.summary: Just a quick update for now. Well, it was too good to last. A free (if dilapidated) marina berth walking distance from town. The bad wiring finally set the lego pontoons alight; luckily extinguished just before the new 75 seat speed boat got caught. So I am quickly out of the place before anything else goes wrong and through the mangroves to Krabi Boat Lago…

items.title: From Phyam back to Krabi
items.summary: Well I rebuilt my self steering (an activity not to be repeated at anchor) and the time came to leave Ko Phyam. Although the wind is offshore there is still a nasty onshore swell from the Indian ocean. Comfortable anchoring places are few and far between and I have to do one 13 hour day. With so much fishing activity night sailing is not an option. I get ba…

items.title: Ko Phyam
items.summary: My application for a long term visa is halted as Immigration decided to stop issuing long term visas for the rest of the year having discovered a huge number of fake visas for Chinese people in Bangkok. Fingers crossed for next year. Work finished and I decide to head off to Ko Phyam, last visited three years ago. So much for the rainy season being over. Pa…

items.title: Singapore 2
items.summary: Krabi immigration have different rules to Phuket 30 miles across the bay. It would take 12 pages to explain it all but suffice to say they will only give me a 30 day visa which cannot be extended. So I have to deposit a bond of about 400 quid, fly out and fly back. Trip number two to Singapore. 1,000 quid all in but at last I manage to buy super tough tyres…

items.title: Hauled out in Krabi
items.summary: Well I can’t stay here for ever and so I head back to Thailand, going to Krabi as the haul out is much cheaper than Phuket. I don’t really need to haul out yet but it is a convenient time to do it. They are dredging the marina but hit a snag as the overground exhaust pipe wont take the pressure. In the meantime my little Thai contractor is driving me nuts.…

items.title: Maha Tower
items.summary: There is lots of politics in Malaysia. They were building this tower as a tribute to a politician and he then went out of favour. The project was put on hold and eventually restarted. From foundations in 2014 the tower opened in September. Nice views of Langkawi and a nice way to spend an hour or two. But Malaysians seem to be unimpressed. On the day I went…

items.title: Cariad
items.summary: Cariad is a teak strip plank boat of over 100 feetfirst built in 1896. My friend DesKearns rebuilt her 14 years ago. A longstory but two owners in a row went under and Cariad lay in a Singapore marina uncaredfor for 13 years. Enter a newbillionaire owner and restoration number two is planned. Des moves his team to Satun in southernThailand and many of the o…

items.title: Leaving Thailand
items.summary: So I arrange to move back to Langkawi and suddenly the Thais change their minds and say I can have another 60 days but by then the weather will be bad and the passage south could be miserable. So I make all the bookings including buying the compulsory Covid insurance and the next day the need for insurance is scrapped. Of course I cannot get my money back.…

items.title: Blog revitalised
items.summary: Well, there has not been a lot going on but I arrived back in Langkawi in early May and got tied up and ready for the rainy season. And rain it did. A daily project is to keep mold at bay but a difficult task on the cordage going up the masts. Many westerners have left Langkawi and some have even left their boats there so all in all things were quite quiet.…
